Arc Flash Study

Find Out What It's All About

For companies of all sizes, Care Labs is a cost-effective source of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services. To ensure that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and thorough, qualified professionals will evaluate the arc flash dangers, staff training needs, documentation requirements, and PPE requirements.

Enquire

What is Involved

Early arc flash risk identification and suitable mitigation of harmonic distortion effects are made possible by Care Labs, ensuring the equipment’s safety. Software called ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) is used to analyze and research arc flashes. 

The amount of heat and light produced by an arc fault is referred to as an arc flash. When current travels via an air gap between conductors, something happens. A significant quantity of light and heat are released by the arc flash, which ionizes the air (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). The process of looking at and analyzing a facility’s electrical installations in order to determine the incident energy that is available for each piece of electrical equipment is known as an arc flash analysis. 

Arc flash studies promote safe working conditions by developing safety standards for qualified electricians working on equipment and circuits. Additionally, updated electrical one-line schematics, equipment locations, a short circuit, coordination analysis, and equipment locations are also included. 

Goals for Arc Flash Research

  • Establish the degree to which the incident energy released during an arc flash fault caused damage. 
  • Changing the protective equipment’s settings to limit exposure to incident energy.
  • Provide safety advice on the threat of arc flash.
  • Obey the laws and regulations that apply to your country.
  • Establish the sort of PPE used at work.

The Negative Effects of Arc Flash

  • Arcs cause serious burns to the skin, face, and eyes.
  • Internal burns to the esophagus and lungs can result from inhaling gases and hot particles.
  • Injuries brought on by flying objects and loose parts

Arc flash studies are crucial for reducing hazards and improving both the overall safety of the company and the safety of electrical staff. The assessment of electrical danger levels and the implementation of appropriate safety measures to reduce the risk of burns and accidents are made possible by arc flash research. Arc flash analysis is required for all utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial enterprises that employ electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switchboards. 

Benefits of Arc Flash Research

  • Improved mechanical and human safety
  • Lower medical and legal costs
  • Adherence to legal and safety requirements
  • A decrease in the frequency and severity of code infractions

The following steps are used for arc flash analysis: 

  • Data gathering
  • Executing site-based verification
  • System planning
  • Examining arc flashes

Carelabs' Arc Flash Research Work Method

CareLabs provides excellent services and will fulfill any requests made by the engineering firm, the local contractor, or the building team. 

The following steps form our work process: 

  • Data gathering
  • Power system modeling with ETAP
  • Examining short-circuits
  • A look into how overcurrent protection devices are coordinated
  • Arc flares are being investigated
  • Carrying out arc flash tests
  • Keeping records
  • Techniques for reducing the risk of an arc flash
  • Large single-line diagrams
  • Displaying arc flash warning signs
  • The complete report

Benefits of Employing CareLabs’ Services: 

  • Create safer electrical systems that follow rules and specifications.
  • Protective devices and bus ratings are paired with integrated modules for comprehensive short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device assessment, and arc flash evaluation.
  • Improving workplace security
  • A quick and simple alternative suggestion for the best design
  • By using user-defined arc fault tolerances, safety margins may be increased.
  • Utilize our computer-assisted analysis to automatically produce arc flash labels to save time.
  • Reduce maintenance expenses and productivity losses.

In order to guarantee that our specialists are available in the event of a routine or urgent crisis, crew members are strategically placed throughout the care laboratories. Care labs have quickly made a name for itself as an ISO 9001:2008-certified company with a customer that has consistently provided positive feedback. 

All major cities, including Paris, Marseille, Lyon, Toulouse, and Nice, are served by our arc flash study and analysis services. Get in touch with us right away for a free arc flash study and analysis estimate.

Harmonic Study and Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

Care Labs offers comprehensive harmonic research and analysis along with electrical installation services such as electrical safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification. To confirm that your electrical safety program is current, compliant, and comprehensive, experts will examine harmonic risks, training programs, paperwork, and personal protective equipment (PPE).

Enquire

What is Involved

To ensure the safety of the equipment, CareLabs can detect harmonic distortions early on and take appropriate measures to lessen their effects. Electrical systems can become inefficient when there are harmonic electric voltages and currents. They generate ineffective heat and have the potential to degrade the quality of power. 

A mathematical method for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels is harmonic voltage analysis. Harmonic frequencies are introduced into the electrical system by nonlinear electrical switching equipment such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, computers, and so on. 

A significant risk of equipment failure is presented by high harmonic voltage/current levels. Therefore, it is crucial to do a quick harmonic analysis and research. Harmonic limitations for systems operating at 415 volts are currently 5%, with 4% for odd harmonics and 2% for even harmonics.

Effects of Harmonic Distortion

  • Causes high voltage and flowing current. 
  • Equipment failure and destruction as a result of extreme voltage distortion. 
  • A rise in the amount of internal energy in components, which causes component failure and a shorter lifespan. 
  • Branch circuit breakers tripping due to faults. 
  • Measuring deviation. 
  • Explosions and flames in the distribution system’s wiring. 
  • Power outages brought on by generators.  
  • A decline in the system’s power factor.  
  • Expensive installation and utility costs.  
  • Productivity losses.  
  • Downtime and unnecessary vehicle trips.  
  • Larger motor size. 

Although harmonic problems are extremely uncommon, when they do occur, they tend to reduce the reliability, power quality, and efficiency of the power system. Therefore, harmonic research and analysis are crucial. 

  • Offers harmonic voltage and current protection. 
  • Voltage and current imbalance. 
  • limitation on frequency fluctuation. 
  • Improve power factor analysis and keep it around one.  
  • Control voltage fluctuations. 

Insight into the likely cause and effects of power quality problems will be provided through harmonic study and analysis, helping to prevent harmonic-related design challenges and reducing the likelihood of unexpected consequences. 

CareLabs' Harmonic Analysis and Research Workflow

  • Highlight from the electrical one-line diagram any possible nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and lengthy medium voltage cables. 
  • Determine the location of the common connection (PCC). 
  • Determine which in-plant system buses are most susceptible to harmonic distortions. 
  • Obtain information about harmonics from all nonlinear loads. 
  • Obtain crucial information on the greatest and lowest short circuit fault levels as well as voltage and current harmonics at the chosen PCC. 
  • Utilize ETAP to conduct a harmonic analysis of the electrical network. 
  • Identify the whole and discrete levels of current and voltage wave distortions at the buses and the PCC. 
  • Draw a spectrum of harmonic frequencies. 
  • An appropriate mitigation solution is suggested and implemented if the harmonic distortion levels are higher than the allowable values. 
  • Perform the analysis again after including the mitigation strategy. 

Benefits of CareLabs Service

Higher ratings are used in the second stage if background harmonics have already been measured. 

  • If the background is less than 25%, stage 2 allows for various converter sizes based on the fault level and converter type.
  • The lower half of the aforementioned converter grade is acceptable if the background is between 25% and 75%.
  • The top background is subjected to a thorough harmonic evaluation.

To guarantee that our experts are accessible in the event of a routine or urgent emergency, CareLabs has crew members stationed in many locations. Care Labs has quickly made a name for itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited business with devoted customers who have provided excellent feedback. 

In a number of important cities in France, such as Paris, Marseille, Lyon, Toulouse, and Nice, we do harmonic research and analysis. Get in touch with us right now to arrange your harmonic analysis and study in France or to get a quote!

  • Carelabz France

Motor Start Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

France businesses may take use of CareLabs’ specialized Motor start study and analysis service. Assessing the unusual consequences of starting a large motor is the main goal of our motor start analysis service. Before turning on an electric motor, it’s important to check for a number of additional factors in addition to the line voltage, such as annoyance tripping, excessive nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, all of which are indicators of a bad power supply. 

Enquire

What is Involved

CareLabs will send qualified personnel to your location to collect recent and previous motor installation data. Then, using the manufacturer’s input and the collected data, calculations are made to predict future effects and give solutions. 

Both static and dynamic simulation techniques may be used to analyze motor-starting procedures 

  • To get the load flow solution, the measured starting impedance of the electric motor is simulated. 
  • Short-circuit technique calculations are utilized for pre-fault short circuit conditions, while voltage drop calculations are used for motor starting currents. 
  • A typical transient stability approach may be used to determine the impact of motor starting if an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available. 
  • Transient stability simulations can be employed when there is a lack of thorough dynamic model data for the electric motor. 

France Requires a Motor Start Analysis

Electric motor sizes and their use in modern industrial systems are both increasing dramatically. The electric motor, locally linked loads, and electrically distant buses can all be disrupted by starting a large motor that is connected to a line. 

The terminal voltage should be at or greater than 80% of the rated voltage when starting an electric motor. Because the square of a motor’s terminal voltage is directly proportional to the torque, voltage difference affects the load torque of the motor. 

Machines that are already in motion may hit their breaking torque and slow down or halt if the voltage drop is considerable. Voltage drop affects, among other things, lighting loads, delicate control systems, and electrical equipment. 

A high starting current also lowers terminal voltage, which raises the current consumed by all systems supplying an ongoing load. Relays with defects from under-voltage are also found. 

When is it appropriate to do a motor start analysis?

  • Prior to or during the purchase of a large motor.
  • In the absence of a generator and when the motor rating is greater than 30% of the supply transformer’s base KVA rating.
  • If the motor rating exceeds 10% to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating and the generator is the only source of power for the system. If a substantial number of motors start at once in an industrial process, a motor start study could be required.
  • Because a smaller power supply has a reduced capacity, problems with voltage loss while starting a high-capacity motor are compounded. The understanding of the output of small generator-containing systems is made easier by the examination of motor beginnings.

Benefits of Motor Start Analysis

Start-up analysis of a motor It is advantageous to use a motor or starting method with the best speed-torque properties. Any exceptional loads must be accelerated under carefully supervised settings in order to prevent going above the equipment’s maximum allowable torque. By doing a motor start analysis, it is possible to examine the motor’s shaft functioning at any moment. As a consequence, it helps in figuring out the equipment’s maximum allowable torque. 

The machine’s voltage and the ability of the electric motor to start and accelerate are determined by motor-starting experiments. To further evaluate your electric motor, CareLabs provides motor start investigations by qualified experts. We provide motor start analysis services in all major France cities, including Paris, Marseille, Lyon, Toulouse, and Nice. You may reserve your motor starting course right now or contact us for a price.

Power quality analysis is the process of assessing the electrical power supply to ensure it meets the required standards for safe and efficient operation of electrical systems.

Read More

Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical method for predicting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on data.

Read More

Motor Start Analysis service helps you to get perspective on the best way of starting motor, and proper power system design for reducing the impact of the motor starting on the power system.

Read More

  • Carelabz France

  • Power Quality Analysis

Power Quality Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs examines the power quality for France businesses. Our goal is to protect your appliances, electrical installations, and electrical equipment from any problems brought on by unstable power supplies. 

Enquire

What is Involved

Today’s data collection, monitoring, and measurement equipment is widely used by several organizations and an increasing number of individual users. This equipment is very susceptible to power supply variations. The voltage fluctuation spectrum, which is a common feature of distributed power, is something that very few electrical equipments are designed to withstand. 

Concerns about equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning may be resolved with the help of CareLabs. 

The Goal of the Power Quality Analysis is to

  • The assessment of differences between the load and the electrical system.
  • Analyzing the workplace to improve simulation methods for providing power quality solutions.
  • Predicting the efficacy and performance of load equipment.
  • Increase the machine’s reliability and power factor.
  • Limit your consumption of energy.
  • To stop malfunctions and disruptions.
  • Reducing power quality anomalies such as transients, spikes, and harmonics.
  • Assist in avoiding PLC hang-ups and voltage/frequency spikes.
  • Among other things, to avoid nuisance tripping, capacitor explosion, and transformer overheating.
  • Significantly cut back on utility costs.
  • Make the electrical network and its parts more durable.
  • Verify the stability of electrical equipment by inspecting it.

Power Quality Analysis Is Necessary

All electrical networks have supply interruptions, which happen more often when power lines are damaged by storms, natural disasters, or accidents. Power supply fluctuations are entirely normal. The characteristics of power supplied by an electricity network are subject to three different forms of fluctuations, disturbances, and distortions. 

  1. When a frequency exceeds its typical frequency range, it deviates from it.
  2. Voltage disruptions occur when the voltage form is maintained but the voltage amplitude is greater than the normal range.
    1.  Short-lived fluctuations include voltage drops (dips) and spikes that last no more than a minute.
    2.  Voltage fluctuations of a lengthy duration are under voltages and over voltages that continue longer than a minute.
    3.  Unbalanced voltage is caused by an unequal load distribution between the phases, which causes significant equipment heating.
    4. Voltage differences between neutral and ground.
  3. Disturbances brought on by voltage distortion, which occurs when the voltage is neither sinusoidal or homogeneous in shape.
    1. Device heating, increased losses, inefficient power utilization, and resonance problems are all caused by harmonics and inter harmonics.
    2.  Short-duration transients that have the potential to harm switch contact surfaces, produce system inefficiency and hasten the breakdown of motor drives and control. 

What is Covered in a Power Quality Analysis?

The following techniques can be used to detect different power quality disruptions:  

  • Harmonic Analysis: This technique makes use of computer algorithms to pinpoint and foresee potential harmonic problems as well as reduction techniques. 
  • Surge and transient analysis: These phenomena will be found in this inquiry. 
  • Analysis of voltage dips and peaks: This study would chart the value and direction of the voltage’s transient rise and fall. 
  • Reactive force evaluation of the preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends will be determined by this investigation. 
  • Analysis of captive electricity to meet demand and reduce energy surcharges and fuel use, this research would measure and synchronize captive power. 
  • Load Flow Analysis: Based on the stated operating parameters, this analysis predicts power flow magnitudes, power factors, voltage levels, and losses in system branches. 

For the benefit of all of its clients, CareLabs is dedicated to providing the best caliber service to improve the caliber and reliability of your electrical installation. In all major cities, including Paris, Marseille, Lyon, Toulouse, and Nice, we provide power quality assessments. Get in touch with us right now to arrange a power quality inspection or to get a quote!

Skip to content
  • Carelabz France
  • Services
  • Power System Study and Analysis

Power System Study and Analysis

Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs offers a range of electrical installation services, including electrical safety inspections, simulations of electrical system designs, studies of power quality, and studies and analyses of power systems. Our team of electrical professionals performs load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies using ETAP, the most efficient tool for power system transient analysis. It enables us to develop and implement the best power compensation and solutions in direct collaboration with clients.

Enquire

Why is a Power System Analysis Required?

Every industrial or commercial building has to have an electrical system that is reliable, consistent, and well-maintained. A power outage of any size can have disastrous effects, extending downtime, damaging essential equipment, and increasing the cost of equipment repair. A periodic study of your facility’s power system might confirm that it is reliable, safe, and efficient in both typical and unusual circumstances. 

An extensive study must be done for a power system at each step of design, installation, and operation. Our power system studies and evaluations will provide an overview of how the system will operate under both normal operation and transient fault conditions using the ETAP software tool. It defines a collection of unique engineering analytical studies used to investigate how an electric power system responds to events occurring over a range of time frames.  

The state of the electricity system at your business has been the subject of several inquiries. A study report on power system analysis may include the following examples: 

  • Circuit Breaker.  
  • Coordination of Security Measures.  
  • The flow of Load.  
  • Dynamic and Transient Assessment.  
  • Arc Flash Investigation 
  • Power Efficiency.  
  • Analysis of Harmonics 
  • launching the engine. 
  • research on earthing. 

It aids businesses in setting benchmarks for other aspects of plant performance generally, such as the electrical safety program. These findings point to the potential for secure and trustworthy operations. 

Analysis of Load Flow

Through the computation of voltages and currents in various network segments and the selection of equipment with the appropriate rating, load flow studies help. The built-in load flow feature of ETAP may be used to perform load flows, which provide data on the power factor, voltages, and currents of a power system. 

Short-Circuit Research

A power system has to do short-circuit research to understand how fault conditions develop. Manually doing short-circuit investigations is a time-consuming task. competent software like ETAP is significantly more effective. 

In order to evaluate the effects of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems, ETAP is used to model system protection devices. 

Quick-Term Study

The amount and types of transient overvoltage and currents caused by lightning strikes and switching operations are determined through studies of transients. The results define how much insulation and surge protection is required. 

Studying the Coordination of Protection

This study aims to validate the various security components in your system. It confirms that they are coordinated and the right size for the machinery they are guarding. 

Our power system analysis process frequently includes the important tasks listed below: 

  • Data gathering process. 
  • Data evaluation and client consultation. 
  • Collecting data on equipment. 
  • Single-line diagram example 
  • Simulating the ETAP power system. 
  • Analysis of load flow and short circuits. 
  • Coordination of protection research. 
  • Analysis of arc flashes. 
  • A summary of the research report. 
  • Analysis of risk minimization and evaluation. 
  • Administration of the project. 

The parts of the CareLabs Power System Analysis are as follows: 

  • Options for reducing energy prices and demand.
  • Reports that point up issues. 
  • Solutions that are suggested. 
  • Knowledge about where and how energy is used. 
  • Ratings for protective equipment are confirmed. 
  • Aid in preventing harm and injury. 
  • Impacts of harmonics and transients. 

Our power system analysis studies focus on analysing the electrical system of the customer and making suggestions on how to increase its efficiency and supply security. One of the major cities where we offer power system analysis and study services is Paris. Other significant cities include Marseille, Lyon, Toulouse, and Nice. Get in touch with us right now to arrange a power system assessment and analysis or to get a quote! 

Related Services

Power System Study & Analysis

Power System Study & Analysis service helps you to have a safe, reliable, and efficient power system for your facility under normal and abnormal circumstances.

Carelabz France
Services
Power System Study and Analysis
Power System Study and Analysis
Find Out What It's All About

CareLabs offers a range of electrical installation services, including electrical safety inspections, simulations of electrical system designs, studies of power quality, and studies and analyses of power systems. Our team of electrical professionals performs load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies using ETAP, the most efficient tool for power system transient analysis. It enables us to develop and implement the best power compensation and solutions in direct collaboration with clients.

Enquire
Why is a Power System Analysis Required?

Every industrial or commercial building has to have an electrical system that is reliable, consistent, and well-maintained. A power outage of any size can have disastrous effects, extending downtime, damaging essential equipment, and increasing the cost of equipment repair. A periodic study of your facility’s power system might confirm that it is reliable, safe, and efficient in both typical and unusual circumstances. 

An extensive study must be done for a power system at each step of design, installation, and operation. Our power system studies and evaluations will provide an overview of how the system will operate under both normal operation and transient fault conditions using the ETAP software tool. It defines a collection of unique engineering analytical studies used to investigate how an electric power system responds to events occurring over a range of time frames.  

The state of the electricity system at your business has been the subject of several inquiries. A study report on power system analysis may include the following examples: 

Circuit Breaker.  
Coordination of Security Measures.  
The flow of Load.  
Dynamic and Transient Assessment.  
Arc Flash Investigation 
Power Efficiency.  
Analysis of Harmonics 
launching the engine. 
research on earthing. 

It aids businesses in setting benchmarks for other aspects of plant performance generally, such as the electrical safety program. These findings point to the potential for secure and trustworthy operations. 

Analysis of Load Flow

Through the computation of voltages and currents in various network segments and the selection of equipment with the appropriate rating, load flow studies help. The built-in load flow feature of ETAP may be used to perform load flows, which provide data on the power factor, voltages, and currents of a power system. 

Short-Circuit Research

A power system has to do short-circuit research to understand how fault conditions develop. Manually doing short-circuit investigations is a time-consuming task. competent software like ETAP is significantly more effective. 

In order to evaluate the effects of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems, ETAP is used to model system protection devices. 

Quick-Term Study

The amount and types of transient overvoltage and currents caused by lightning strikes and switching operations are determined through studies of transients. The results define how much insulation and surge protection is required. 

Studying the Coordination of Protection

This study aims to validate the various security components in your system. It confirms that they are coordinated and the right size for the machinery they are guarding. 

Our power system analysis process frequently includes the important tasks listed below: 

Data gathering process. 
Data evaluation and client consultation. 
Collecting data on equipment. 
Single-line diagram example 
Simulating the ETAP power system. 
Analysis of load flow and short circuits. 
Coordination of protection research. 
Analysis of arc flashes. 
A summary of the research report. 
Analysis of risk minimization and evaluation. 
Administration of the project. 

The parts of the CareLabs Power System Analysis are as follows: 

Options for reducing energy prices and demand.
Reports that point up issues. 
Solutions that are suggested. 
Knowledge about where and how energy is used. 
Ratings for protective equipment are confirmed. 
Aid in preventing harm and injury. 
Impacts of harmonics and transients. 

Our power system analysis studies focus on analysing the electrical system of the customer and making suggestions on how to increase its efficiency and supply security. One of the major cities where we offer power system analysis and study services is Paris. Other significant cities include Marseille, Lyon, Toulouse, and Nice. Get in touch with us right now to arrange a power system assessment and analysis or to get a quote!
